name: Cumulonimbus Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour two 8-inch baking pans.
3. Rinse and drain the sauerkraut in a sieve; snip fine and set aside.
4. In a bowl, combine the flour, cocoa powder, baking soda, and salt.
5. In a large mixing bowl, beat the butter, sugar, and vanilla extract with an electric mixer until well combined.
6. Add the eggs, beating after each addition.
7. Add the dry ingredients alternately with the coffee, beating until just combined.
8. Stir in the sauerkraut by hand.
9. Pour into the prepared pans.
10. Bake for 30 to 35 minutes, or until done.
11. Cool in the pans for 10 minutes.
12. Remove and cool completely.
Chocolate-Sour Cream Frosting:
1. Pound milk chocolate.
2. In a heavy saucepan over very low heat, melt the chocolate.
3. Cool for 10 minutes.
4. Stir in the sour cream; the mixture may stiffen at first but will become smooth as you stir in all of the sour cream.
